title: EU should make 'individual assessments' before returning migrants, says UNHCR representative
content: Jean-Nicolas Beuze, UNHCR representative in Brussels, believes it is key for the EU to ensure "nobody is returning to a possible situation of harm" when implementing the recently approved Pact on Migration and Asylum.

title: NATO summit: Europe signals it's ready to take control of its own defence

title: Exclusive: Brussels puts trade ban with Israeli settlements on the table

title: World Cup sparks wave of racist misinformation online
content: False claims and AI-generated videos with racist overtones have circulated during the FIFA World Cup alongside genuine incidents of racism and xenophobia.

title: March for peace in memory of the victims of the Srebrenica genocide

title: Tanks vs. tractors? Can the EU keep providing a political shield to farmers? MEPs debate in The Ring
content: Tough talks on the EU’s next long-term budget, Ukraine’s push for EU accession and skyrocketing fertiliser costs are putting European farmers under the political spotlight once more. Can the EU continue to appease them as it shifts its focus to defence and global trade? MEPs debate on The Ring.

title: Ukraine opens criminal probe after crowds attack army conscription vehicle in Lviv

title: Western Europe recorded hottest June as heatwaves surge, EU's climate monitor says

snippet: Global temperatures in June were 1.39C above the estimated pre-industrial average, a period covering 1850-1900, according to Copernicus.

title: Up to 38°C at the weekend: how long will this heatwave last in Germany?

title: NATO summit ended 'on a more positive note than feared', former NATO security advisor says
content: Jamie Shea, former NATO security adviser, says NATO 3.0 is going to be "much more a European show and less an American show" than it used to be. According to Shea, the US "is going to do a lot less in terms of holding up the collective defence than allies were used to during the Cold War".

title: From sewers to swimming sites: how Europe's cities reclaim their rivers

title: Eight member states oppose the opening of Serbia’s next EU accession cluster
